Marc David Spengler.additionalHistoryText: Web research (verify): Born 1995; based in Stuttgart, Germany. Studied communication design at Staatliche Akademie der Bildenden Künste Stuttgart, diploma 2022. Studio active as freelance illustrator/designer since Oct 2015 (LinkedIn). Clients named on studio site include Hermès, Google, The New York Times, Warby Parker, The Atlantic, Vans, Adobe, and others. Represented by MUCH Creative (North America) and Ampersand Global (China). Work described as spanning national and international exhibitions; named press includes Creative Boom, WePresent, Stuttgarter Zeitung, and Collater.al (confirm URLs and dates on primary sources).
Marc David Spengler.exhibitionsText:
2022 — Diploma, communication design, Staatliche Akademie der Bildenden Künste Stuttgart, Stuttgart, Germany (verify show title)
2015 — Freelance studio practice begins, Stuttgart area, Germany (per LinkedIn; not a gallery show)

Moritz Adam Schmitt.additionalHistoryText: Web research (verify): Cologne-based illustrator and designer. BA Mediendesign, Rheinische Fachhochschule Köln, graduated 2016. Broke out via year-long “One Icon a Day” on Instagram (~22k followers) and Adobe MAX 2016 invitation (Adobe Blog). Represented by kombinatrotweiss-illustration; clients cited include Adobe, Microsoft, Xbox, Samsung, Porsche, Bosch (Behance/rep sources). Influences cited in rep interviews include Banksy, Warhol, Basquiat, Haring.
Moritz Adam Schmitt.exhibitionsText:
2016 — Adobe MAX participant, San Diego, USA (industry event; verify role)
2016 — BA graduation showcase context, Rheinische Fachhochschule Köln, Cologne, Germany (verify)

Loreta Isac.additionalHistoryText: Web research (verify): Bucharest-based illustrator and animator; represented by Agent Pekka. Resume page lists solo shows in Bucharest (2011–2016) and group shows including Romanian Design Week (2013–2018), Timișoara, Wrocław, Beijing, Amsterdam (see exhibitions list). Clients named on rep page include Apple, Sam Smith, Bon Appétit, The Washington Post, The New York Times (verify current roster).

Dawal.additionalHistoryText: Web research (verify): Self-taught French street artist based in Paris; born 1994. Began painting in streets around 18–19, influenced by hip-hop, moving from lettering toward character-driven work (Posca interview). Open studio at Shakirail, Paris 18e, since Oct 2023. Practice includes murals, canvas, drawing, sculpture; transforms wall imperfections into miniature dreamlike scenes (Creapills and other press).
Dawal.exhibitionsText:
2026 — Solo, Rêve Lucide, Galerie Artistik Rezo, Paris, France
2024 — Solo, Enfantillages, Galerie Artistik Rezo, Paris, France

Hedof.additionalHistoryText: Web research (verify): Hedof is the studio of Dutch illustrator Rick Berkelmans, based in Breda (also active Amsterdam per dutchculture.nl). Pictoplasma conference listing and dutchculture.nl note group exhibitions in Shanghai (2021) and Seoul (2019), plus lectures/fairs in Brazil and China. Clients named on about page include Nike, The New York Times, Ikea, Google, PlayStation, and many others (self-reported list—verify for live campaigns).
Hedof.exhibitionsText:
2021 — Group exhibition context, Shanghai, China (per dutchculture.nl; verify title/venue)

Yonil.additionalHistoryText: Web research (verify): Jonathan Lax, professionally YONIL, Tel Aviv-based artist/illustrator/designer, born 1982. Work spans posters, prints, personal art, music-industry commissions; publications cited include IdN and Illustration Now! (yonil.com/about). Behance presence with large follower count; Braverman Gallery lists 2010 group show participation.
Yonil.exhibitionsText: 2010 — Group, Cnaan, Braverman Gallery, Tel Aviv, Israel

Taloosh.additionalHistoryText: Web research (verify): Taloosh Studio, Haifa, Israel—creative studio working on branding, promos, murals, and exhibition-related projects. Regional documentation references exhibition tour activity at Beit Hankin Gallery (May 2023) and multiple branding clients (Hamamat Tarbut 2023, Hamsin Magazine 2022, etc.—verify on taloosh.com pages).
Taloosh.exhibitionsText: 2023 — Exhibition tour / gallery programming, Beit Hankin Gallery, Israel (verify city; source: regional doc summary)

Troy Browne.additionalHistoryText: Web research (verify): Nottingham-based UK artist; collage, motion design, dynamic composition. BSc Multimedia, Nottingham Trent University, 2007–2010. Freelance via Troy Browne Motion Design since Dec 2018. Signed with The Different Folk (Oct 2022). Clients cited include Sony Music, Vimeo, Universal Music, ITV, BBC, Crocs, Dropbox, and others (Different Folk / event pages—verify).
Troy Browne.exhibitionsText: 2025 — Speaker, Confetti Industry Week, Nottingham and London, UK (industry week; verify format as talk vs exhibition)

Elfassi.additionalHistoryText: Web research (verify): Israeli illustrator/designer; BA Visual Communication (illustration), Shenkar College, Ramat Gan. Practice includes hand and digital drawing, embroidery, murals; clients named on site include hospitality and footwear brands (verify current list). Public project pages reference mural work (e.g., Talpiot area, 2023 per aggregator thisispublic—confirm).
Elfassi.exhibitionsText: 2023 — Mural, Talpiot district, Jerusalem, Israel (verify; source: third-party mural page)

Emelio Cerezo.additionalHistoryText: Web research (verify): Emilio Cerezo, born 1986 Totana (Murcia), based Barcelona. Graffiti/street aliases 2001–2012; working under full name from 2012. Fine arts University of Granada 2004–2009; postgraduate drawing 2009–2010. Practice spans painting, murals, tattooing (UrbanPresents / KIFP summaries).
Emelio Cerezo.exhibitionsText:
2018 — Group, KEEP IT FRESH CHAPTER 1, Amsterdam, Netherlands
2017 — Group, Possessed Spirits, Amsterdam, Netherlands

Ori Toor.additionalHistoryText: Web research (verify): Tel Aviv illustrator, fine artist, animator. Studied illustration at Shenkar College; prior newspaper layout work. Known for improvisational “Gibberish” series and commercial work for Nike, Wired, Cartoon Network; Adobe Illustrator 2018 splash screen; Glas Animation Festival 2018 poster (portfolio sources). D&AD Illustration Jury 2021.

Antonia Lev.additionalHistoryText: Web research (verify): Paris-based illustrator and street artist; Behance since 2011; themes include lovers and plants; tools include Procreate and spray paint. Festival/mural projects include MESTO 2020 (Nizhny Novgorod) and 2025 work for MURAL CITY / suburban Paris (Street Art Cities marker—verify titles).
